author: Dr. Marcus Reilly, MD
content:
ORTHOPEDIC PRE-OPERATIVE CONSULT — 3/22/2018, 10:30 AM
Patient: Nguyen, Lan Thi (Age 63)
Referring physician: Dr. Phong Le (Charm City Asian Health Center)
Interpreter: Hopkins in-person Vietnamese interpreter.

REASON FOR VISIT:
Evaluation for right total knee arthroplasty (TKA). Patient referred for progressive right-knee osteoarthritis no longer adequately managed with conservative therapy.

HPI:
63-year-old Vietnamese-speaking woman with ~10 years of progressive right knee pain. Currently still working as floor manager at a textile factory (Highlandtown), which involves standing 6–8 hours per shift. Right knee pain is now constant, worse with weight-bearing and stairs, interfering with her work and her tai chi. Has tried acetaminophen, ibuprofen (up to 1600 mg/day on bad days), physical therapy (6-week course 2016), intra-articular corticosteroid injection (2016, ~3 months of relief), and a hinged knee brace. Hyaluronic acid injection series (2017, Synvisc) provided modest benefit lasting ~4 months. She would like to proceed with TKA before her 65th-birthday Medicare transition because her current PPO coverage is more favorable.

FUNCTIONAL STATUS: Independent ADLs. Walks ~1 mile per day in aggregate (work + errands). Difficulty with stairs at home (3rd-floor apartment). Modified her tai chi practice — does seated forms only on bad-knee days. KOOS knee survey score ~52 (severe).

PMH:
- Hypertension — well-controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily
- Hyperlipidemia — on atorvastatin 40 mg daily (LDL recent ~95)
- Right knee OA (this) — KL grade 4 medial compartment on plain films
- Latent TB infection (positive PPD/IGRA 2010, never treated; asymptomatic)
- Former smoker (quit 2004, ~14 pack-years lifetime)
- Post-menopausal

MEDS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily
- Multivitamin daily
- Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO TID PRN
- Ibuprofen 400–800 mg PO PRN (1–2x/week) — counseled to hold 10 days pre-op

ALLERGIES: Penicillin (childhood rash — non-anaphylactic). NKDA otherwise.

SOCIAL: Married to Hai (age 67, CHF, declining). Lives with husband and adult son Quang (age 38) in a 3rd-floor walk-up. Plan post-op: son's family (younger son's family — Quang and his wife Linh) will host her at their home in Charles Village for 4–6 weeks recovery, which has an elevator. Husband Hai will not be able to assist with care given his own illness; son's family will primary-care him too during that time.

VITALS: BP 138/82, HR 80, RR 14, T 98.4 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 61.8 kg (136 lb). BMI 26.1.

RIGHT KNEE EXAM:
- Inspection: mild varus alignment, no effusion grossly, no erythema, well-healed prior arthroscopy port scars (none, sorry — no prior surgery).
- Palpation: medial-joint-line tenderness ++, lateral mild +, patellar grind +.
- ROM: 5° to 110° (limited extension; full extension is 0°).
- Stability: ligaments intact (negative Lachman, valgus/varus stress stable).
- Gait: antalgic right.

IMAGING REVIEWED:
- Right knee X-rays today (PA / lateral / sunrise): KL grade 4 medial-compartment joint-space loss with bone-on-bone. Mild patellofemoral involvement. Osteophytes. No fracture. No suspicious lesions.
- LEFT knee X-rays today: KL grade 2 changes; not surgical at this time.

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. SEVERE END-STAGE OSTEOARTHRITIS, RIGHT KNEE — appropriate candidate for TKA.
2. Pre-op clearance: cardiology (given husband's CHD history makes patient anxious; we want a screening ECG + risk stratification given HTN + HLD). Internal medicine clearance from Dr. Le.
3. Pre-op labs: CBC, CMP, coag, type & screen, UA.
4. Latent TB — discussed with patient and interpreter that TKA risk of TB reactivation is low (no biologic / TNFi exposure). Will not delay surgery for INH treatment. Will continue to monitor symptoms.
5. Anticoagulation post-op: planned aspirin 81 mg BID x 4 weeks per Hopkins TKA protocol (her DVT risk is moderate; no prior VTE; no malignancy; mobile patient).
6. Pre-op education in Vietnamese (handouts) provided. Discussed: surgical risks (infection, bleeding, DVT/PE, anesthesia complications, prosthetic failure, neurovascular injury), realistic outcomes (significant pain relief expected, ~95% durability at 10 years).
7. Scheduled for right TKA on 4/12/2018 with myself as surgeon. Pre-admission testing 4/3/2018.
8. Husband's care: social work consult arranged to ensure home support for husband Hai during patient's hospital stay and rehab — son's wife has volunteered.

Visit time: 70 minutes (interpreter time 40 minutes).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Marcus Reilly, MD
Orthopedic Surgery — Johns Hopkins Bayview
Date: 3/22/2018
